description:
WHY: close_and_archive in src/store.rs has a two-block parent-bookkeeping pattern that breaks when the parent task has already been archived from the state branch. The first block guards correctly: `if let Ok(mut parent) = self.load_task(pid)` silently skips when load fails because the parent JSON is gone. But the second block, after the state-worktree lock is taken, unconditionally runs `git_add` on the parent's JSON+notes paths. If the parent file does not exist in the state worktree, git add fails with pathspec did not match and the entire close aborts.

REPRO: child task whose `parent` field points at an epic id that has already been closed (its JSON archived from balls/tasks). Running `bl close <child>` from the repo root fails on the parent's missing JSON, even though the delivery commit is already on main; only the archival step is stuck. Workaround: `bl update <child> parent=` to detach the dead parent, then `bl close`.

SHAPE: tie the two blocks together so the post-lock `git_add` only stages parent files when the parent actually loaded (and was re-saved). When the parent is archived, there is nowhere to record closed_children anyway, so silently skipping parent bookkeeping is the right behavior. The accompanying source comment that claims the parent file 'always exists post-`save_task`' should be tightened: it is true conditional on the load succeeding.

TEST: add a regression in tests/close_drop.rs (or sibling) — create parent epic + child, close the parent, then close the child; assert the child close succeeds and the child's task file is archived.

OBSERVED: bl-9e8a (child of bl-41ee, both delivered) tripped this. f4f827e was already on main.
title: bl close fails when child's --parent epic is archived
